title: Reading and Use of English – Part 3
description: For questions 17–24, read the text below. Use the word given in CAPITALS at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.

content.text: Reading is (0) WIDELY recognized as a beneficial habit for all ages. It provides (17) stimulation that can help slow the progress of cognitive decline. Many people find (18) in losing themselves in a fictional world. This (19) allows individuals to manage their stress levels (20). Moreover, reading (21) our vocabulary and improves our writing skills. There is a great (22) that avid readers will have better communication abilities. (23) of the genre, whether it is history or fantasy, the (24) gained is invaluable for personal development. It opens up new perspectives and fosters empathy by allowing us to experience lives very different from our own. In an increasingly digital world, the simple act of turning a page remains one of the most effective ways to sharpen the mind and broaden one's understanding of the world.

example.explanation: Adverb required to modify the verb 'recognized'; WIDE → WIDELY.

questions.explanation: Adjective required to modify 'stimulation'; MIND → MENTAL.
Noun required after the verb 'find'; ENJOY → ENJOYMENT.
Verb in present simple third person required; RICH → ENRICHES.
